# Domain Glossary

<!-- Auto-generated by /domain-modeling. A glossary, not a spec. -->

## Organization Hierarchy

The SDA denomination uses a tiered structure. Theobase models these org units:

- **Division** — largest territorial unit (e.g., Inter-American Division). Oversees Unions.
- **Union** — grouping of Conferences/Missions within a Division.
- **Conference** — grouping of Churches and Companies. May be called a **Mission** or **Field** in some territories; modeled as the same org level. Holds authority to organize new Churches.
- **District** — a pastoral grouping of multiple Churches and/or Companies sharing one pastor. Orthogonal to the org hierarchy; not a governance level. A District groups congregations, but membership authority remains with each Church.
- **Local Church** (or just "Church") — a fully organized congregation. Holds its own church book. Votes on membership reception, transfer, and removal. Sends delegates to Conference constituency sessions.
- **Company** — an organized group of believers recognized by the Conference but not yet a full Church. Does **not** hold its own church book; members are carried on the parent Church's or Conference church's book. Limited governance (no elder ordination authority, no delegates).
- **Branch** — a satellite group attached to a parent Church (often from a Sabbath School that grew). Not a separate org unit; modeled as a property of the parent Church.

_Aliases_: "Mission" = Conference level. "Field" = Conference level. "Congregation" = Church or Company.

_Relationships_: Division contains Unions. Union contains Conferences. Conference contains Churches, Companies, and Districts. District groups Churches and Companies (many-to-many possible: one Church may belong to only one District at a time). Branch belongs to exactly one Church.

## Membership

- **Baptized Member** — a person received into membership by profession of faith and baptism. Appears on exactly one Church's book. Has voting rights and may hold office.
- **Membership States** (enum values for a Member's status):
  - `active` — Baptized member in good standing on this Church's book.
  - `under-censure` — Disciplinary state. Retains membership but restricted from office, voting, teaching, leading. Temporary and redemptive.
  - `transferred-out` — Transfer letter granted by sending Church, not yet accepted by receiving Church. Still belongs to sending Church until acceptance.
  - `transferred-in` — Receiving Church has voted to accept; awaiting sending Church's letter. (Or: receiving has the letter, processing completion.)
  - `disfellowshipped` — Formal excommunication by Church body vote with due process (Matthew 18:15-17).
  - `apostasy` — Joined another religious body or publicly repudiated church beliefs. Administrative removal by Church board.
  - `missing` — Cannot be located after diligent effort (2+ years). Administrative removal by Church board. No moral judgment.
  - `renounced` — Voluntarily requested removal from church book. Administrative board vote.
  - `deceased` — Membership terminated by death.
- **Church Book** — the authoritative membership roll maintained by the Church clerk. Only a Church holds a church book; a Company does not.
- **Transfer Letter** — the official document moving membership between Churches. Valid for a limited period (default 6 months). If not accepted by receiving Church within the window, the member reverts to the sending Church's book.
- **Vote of Censure** — a formal reprimand adopted by the Church body. Not a removal — the member stays on the book with restricted privileges.

_Constraint_: A Member belongs to exactly one Church at any point in time.

## Roles

- **Member** — a baptized member with limited write access to their own record. Can:
  - Update own contact info, address, phone, household composition
  - Submit giving declarations (tithe/offerings subject to Treasurer verification)
  - Request own transfer out (clerk reviews, board votes)
  - Request baptism (or profession of faith)
  - Self-report relocated/missing
  - Confirm annual roll (annual "I'm still here" check-in)
  - Mark Sabbath School class attendance (if teacher)
  - Log service/outreach hours
  - Register for church events
  - **Proxy entry**: enter data on behalf of another member in the same Church (tagged "entered by X on behalf of Y"). Every proxy entry is auditable. Verification steps still apply for financial entries.
- **Clerk** — maintains the church book, processes transfers, compiles statistical reports. Approves/oversees member-submitted changes.
- **Treasurer** — receives and disburses funds, verifies giving declarations against physical envelopes/online payments, maintains financial records, produces contribution statements.
- **Pastor** — ordained minister assigned to one or more Churches (often via a District). Read access to all data for their District; write access per board decisions.
- **Elder** — lay leader authorized to conduct services, visit members, assist in governance. Read-only on church data.
- **Conference Secretary** — oversees membership records and statistical reporting at the Conference level. Read/write across all Churches in Conference; approves transfers.
- **Conference Treasurer** — oversees financial compliance and consolidation at the Conference level. Read/write financial across Conference; remittance reconciliation.
- **Union/Division Officer** — read-only aggregation across all sub-orgs in territory.
- **System Admin** — global scope. Org unit management, user provisioning, policy configuration.

## Financial

- **Tithe** — 10% of a member's income. "Holy to the Lord." A single category, not subdivided. 100% remitted to the Conference. The local church does not retain or spend tithe. Funds pastoral/evangelistic labor at Conference level and above.
- **Offering** — voluntary giving beyond tithe. Four categories per the Personal Giving Plan:
  - **Local Church Budget** — for local church operations (utilities, supplies, staff, insurance). Stays local. Suggested: 3-5% of income.
  - **Conference Advance** — for Conference-level ministries (education, evangelism, camps). Remitted to Conference. Suggested: 1-2%.
  - **World Budget / Sabbath School** — for General Conference world mission. Includes the weekly Sabbath School offering and the quarterly Thirteenth Sabbath Offering. Remitted to GC via Conference. Suggested: 1-3%.
  - **Special Projects** — designated for specific causes (building, mission trips, ministries). Direction varies by designation.
- **Fund** — a named pool of money with a direction (local or upstream) and optional destination org level. Each financial transaction is tagged to a Fund.
- **Receipt** — money received (tithe envelope, offering, donation). Recorded in the Cash Receipts Journal.
- **Disbursement** — money paid out (check issued, expense paid). Recorded in the Cash Disbursements Journal.
- **Ingathering** — annual world mission fundraising campaign. Collected locally, remitted to Conference.
- **Building Fund** — segregated capital fund for construction/renovation. Stays local.
- **Donor** — a person who gives tithe/offerings. Tracked with numbered envelopes. Receives an annual contribution statement for tax purposes.
- **Contribution Statement** — annual receipt issued to each donor listing all contributions by category, required for tax compliance. Includes: church name/EIN, donor name/address, date and amount per contribution, total, and "no goods/services provided" statement.
- **Treasurer's Records** — Cash Receipts Journal, Cash Disbursements Journal, General Ledger (chart of accounts), monthly bank reconciliations, tithe/offering envelope records, board minutes, annual financial statements.
- **Chart of Accounts** — standardized by the SDA Accounting Manual (GC/NAD Treasury). Uniform account codes across SDA churches.

_Constraint_: Tithe cannot be spent locally — it is always remitted 100% to the Conference. Offerings may be local or upstream depending on category.

<!-- CONTINUING — more terms will be added as the grilling session proceeds -->

## Reporting

- Reports are **generated**, not submitted. Local officers enter discrete events (a baptism, a transfer vote, an attendance count, a tithe deposit). Quarterly/annual statistical reports and financial summaries are queries over accumulated data — no separate "fill out the QSR" step.
- Conference/Union/Division users can slice and export reports from live aggregated data at any time, in any form they choose. No burden on local officers beyond accurate data entry.

## Offline

- **Online-first** model. The Worker (Cloudflare Workers + D1) is the source of truth.
- The PWA client uses a Service Worker for aggressive local caching (reads cached, writes cached and queued to network).
- When offline: data entry continues locally. Changes sync when connectivity returns.
- Sync conflicts: last-write-wins with server-side audit log. Safe because a single Church typically has one clerk on one device.
- Upstream users (Conference/Union/Division) always see server state, which is the freshest available data.
